questions.text: Walk me through a DCF, including how you arrive at terminal value and what discount rate you use.
Walk me through the three financial statements and how they connect to one another.
What is the difference between enterprise value and equity value? Show me the bridge between them.
If depreciation increases by ten dollars and the tax rate is 30 percent, walk me through the impact on each of the three statements.
Which valuation methodology typically yields the highest value, and why?
When would you not use a DCF, and what would you use instead?
Walk me through how a leveraged buyout works and what makes a good LBO candidate.
A company issues 50 million dollars in debt to buy back stock. What happens to enterprise value and equity value?
How would you value a company that has no current revenue but a strong product?
Explain WACC and walk me through how you would calculate it for a public company.
A client is considering acquiring a competitor for 500 million dollars. How would you assess whether the price is fair?
A retail company wants to go public in 12 months. Walk me through the steps you would advise them to take.
Two SaaS companies have identical revenue but different growth rates. How would the valuation multiples differ and why?
Why investment banking, and why this firm specifically?
Tell me about a time you had to deliver under extreme deadline pressure. What was your approach?
Walk me through your resume in two minutes.
Explain working capital and walk through how changes in receivables, payables, and inventory affect cash flow.
What is the difference between accretive and dilutive M&A? How do you tell quickly in an interview setting?
Walk me through a comparable companies analysis. How do you decide which companies belong in the set?
What is the difference between LIFO and FIFO inventory accounting, and which one signals a more conservative balance sheet?
A private equity client is considering an investment in a regional grocery chain. What questions would you want answered before you advise?
Walk me through how you would structure a sell-side process for a profitable family-owned business.
Describe a time you had to deliver bad news to a senior stakeholder.
Tell me about a deal or transaction you followed closely. What made it interesting?
What does it mean when a company has negative working capital? Is that always bad?
